syntheticDeclaration.purpose: SYNTHETIC TEST FIXTURE. Exercises the young-infant scope exit (built-in intervals begin at 6 months) to prove the engine does not silently apply a fallback interval below that boundary. All values are invented for schema and safety-test purposes only.

executionBinding.engineRationale: Age-scope exit is expressible as a patient-input.schema.json input (ageMonths below 6) and evaluated by assessPediatricAnemia.

expectedBehavior.requiredSignal: Age/developmental partitions outside validated scope must not use fallback thresholds silently. Visible scope exit or abstention.
forbiddenSignals: a resolved hemoglobinLowerLimit, mcvLowerLimit, or mcvUpperLimit for a 2-month-old (no built-in interval covers ages under 6 months)
an anemiaStatus other than indeterminate when no local range is supplied for an age below 6 months

a returned assessment result of any kind (with or without alerts) for an age outside the supported range with no local reference limits supplied — docs/architecture.md ARCH §10 requires a hard refusal ('no assessment produced'), not a returned indeterminate/empty result

expectedTrace.note: EP5-T6 (2026-07-21) supersedes the original 2026-07-19 execution note below. Re-verified by direct execution: assessPediatricAnemia() now throws AgeOutOfSupportedRangeError (code AGE_OUT_OF_SUPPORTED_RANGE, statusCode 400) for this exact input before any rule ever evaluates -- no result object, no provenance.matchedRuleIds, no rule_audit trail exists at all, hence mechanism 'none_available'. SCOPE-001/SCOPE-003 no longer fire for this scenario because assess() refuses before reaching rule evaluation; they remain witnessed elsewhere (tests/witness/alerts/scope-neonatal-young-infant.json, now with cbc.localRanges supplied so the refusal's documented carve-out applies and the module proceeds to fire the alerts). ORIGINAL 2026-07-19 note (behavior superseded, kept for the audit trail): classification.thresholdSource/ageBand/hemoglobinLowerLimit were all null and anemiaStatus/morphology were 'indeterminate' for a 2-month-old with no local ranges supplied via a normally-returned result carrying SCOPE-001/SCOPE-003 alerts; EP5-T6 determined that ARCH §10's 'refuse to assess (not merely narrow limitations text)' requirement was not met by that softer abstention and hardened it to a genuine refusal.

rollbackSignal: Re-verify by execution if modules/anemia/rules.json, candidates.json, or module.json digests change, or if the supportedAge boundary (now read from modules/anemia/module.json's supportedAgeMonths, not hardcoded), modules/anemia/facts.anemia.js#assertAgeWithinSupportedScope, or SCOPE-001/SCOPE-003 are edited.
